entry.title: Inside Olympic Cybersecurity: Lessons From Paris 2024 to Milan Cortina 2026
entry.content:
Discover how Franz Regul, former CISO for the Paris 2024 Olympics, tackled unique cybersecurity challenges to protect the Games from evolving threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: GlassWorm Malware Evolves to Hide in Dependencies
entry.content:
Researchers have identified dozens of malicious GlassWorm extensions that come with new evasion techniques.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: China-Nexus Hackers Skulk in Southeast Asian Military Orgs for Years
entry.content:
Researchers uncovered an extensive cyber espionage campaign that used novel backdoors and familiar evasion techniques to maintain persistent access to regional targets.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Warlock Ransomware Group Augments Post-Exploitation Activities
entry.content:
In a recent attack, the group showcased stealthier cross-network activity, thanks to its use of a new BYOVD technique and other tools.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
entry.content:
In an unsuccessful phishing attack, threat actors leveraged trusted brands and domains to try to redirect a C-suite executive at Outpost24 to give up his credentials.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Less Lucrative Ransomware Market Makes Attackers Alter Methods
entry.content:
Ransomware actors are ditching Cobalt Strike in favor of native Windows tools, as payment rates hit record lows and data theft surges.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Clear Communication: The Missing Link in Cybersecurity Success
entry.content:
When technical expertise meets clear communication, cybersecurity teams thrive. Learn how to foster trust and collaboration across diverse working groups.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: More Attackers Are Logging In, Not Breaking In
entry.content:
Credential theft soared in the second half of 2025, thanks in part to the industrialization of infostealer malware and AI-enabled social engineering.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 'Claudy Day’ Trio of Flaws Exposes Claude Users to Data Theft
entry.content:
A prompt injection vulnerability paired with other flaws can turn a Google search into a full attack chain that could threaten enterprise networks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: DarkSword: iPhone Exploit Kit Serves Spies & Thieves Alike
entry.content:
A sophisticated iOS exploit chain leverages multiple zero-day vulnerabilities and is targeting users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Apple Debuts Background Security Improvements With Fresh WebKit Patches
entry.content:
The lightweight updates are meant to deliver security protections between security updates. The post Apple Debuts Background Security Improvements With Fresh WebKit Patches appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iranian Hackers Likely Used Malware-Stolen Credentials in Stryker Breach
entry.content:
The medtech giant has been working on restoring systems affected by the cyberattack conducted by the Handala hackers. The post Iranian Hackers Likely Used Malware-Stolen Credentials in Stryker Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Shadow AI Risk: How SaaS Apps Are Quietly Enabling Massive Breaches
entry.content:
From Chaos to Control examines the chaos that often comes from shadow AI hidden in SaaS apps and urges better visibility and control over agentic AI. The post Shadow AI Risk: How SaaS Apps Are Quietly Enabling Massive Breaches appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: EU Sanctions Chinese, Iranian Firms Supporting Hacking Operations
entry.content:
The sanctions target two Chinese individuals, two Chinese companies, and one Iranian firm involved in hacking EU member states. The post EU Sanctions Chinese, Iranian Firms Supporting Hacking Operations appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘DarkSword’ iOS Exploit Kit Used by State-Sponsored Hackers, Spyware Vendors
entry.content:
Targeting six iOS vulnerabilities and leading to full device compromise, the exploit chain is meant for surveillance. The post ‘DarkSword’ iOS Exploit Kit Used by State-Sponsored Hackers, Spyware Vendors appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cloud Security Startup Native Exits Stealth With $42 Million in Funding
entry.content:
Phil Venables, former CISO of Google Cloud and now a venture partner at Ballistic Ventures, has joined Native’s board of directors. The post Cloud Security Startup Native Exits Stealth With $42 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Autonomous Offensive Security Firm XBOW Raises $120M at $1B+ Valuation
entry.content:
The company has developed an AI-powered platform that autonomously discovers and validates software vulnerabilities. The post Autonomous Offensive Security Firm XBOW Raises $120M at $1B+ Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: The Collapse of Predictive Security in the Age of Machine-Speed Attacks
entry.content:
With exploitation of vulnerabilities taking just days, preemptive security must be the new model for defenders. The post The Collapse of Predictive Security in the Age of Machine-Speed Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CISA official says agency has not seen uptick in cyber threats amid Iran war
entry.content:
Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security (CISA) Acting Director Nick Andersen said the agency has been working closely with industry and sector-based groups on threats from Iran in the past couple of weeks.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Bank software vendor Marquis says more than 670,000 impacted by August breach
entry.content:
The company, which provides software that allows financial institutions to communicate with customers, previously warned in November that at least 74 banks, credit unions and financial institutions were impacted by a data breach.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: US intelligence chief grilled on absence of election threats in security assessment
entry.content:
Previous clandestine community assessments have documented, at least at a high level, attempts by Iran, Russia or China to sway voters with online propaganda or through cyber operations.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russia-linked hackers use advanced iPhone exploit to target Ukrainians
entry.content:
The malware, dubbed DarkSword, allows attackers to break into iPhones with little to no user interaction, extract sensitive data within minutes, and then erase traces of the intrusion, researchers at cybersecurity firm Lookout said.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
entry.content:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Long Island Plastic Surgical Group Settles Class Action Lawsuit Over BlackCat Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
A consolidated class action lawsuit against Long Island Plastic Surgical Group, P.C has been resolved with a $2,600,000 settlement. Legal action […] The post Long Island Plastic Surgical Group Settles Class Action Lawsuit Over BlackCat Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: California Dental Care Provider Announces Data Breach
entry.content:
A data breach has been announced by Tieu Dental Corporation in California. The Children’s Council of San Francisco has determined […] The post California Dental Care Provider Announces Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Ransomware Group Claims Attacks on Meadowlark Hills Retirement Community & MedPeds
entry.content:
Meadowlark Hills retirement community in Kansas and MedPeds Associates of Sarasota in Florida have announced data breaches. The Beast ransomware […] The post Ransomware Group Claims Attacks on Meadowlark Hills Retirement Community & MedPeds appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: CommonSpirit Health Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach
entry.content:
The Chicago, IL-based Catholic health system CommonSpirit Health has announced that it has been affected by a security incident at […] The post CommonSpirit Health Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: PHI Exposed in Data Breaches at Cedar Valley Services; Community Nurse; Health Dimensions Group
entry.content:
Data breaches have recently been reported by Cedar Valley Services and Health Dimensions Group in Minnesota, and Community Nurse in […] The post PHI Exposed in Data Breaches at Cedar Valley Services; Community Nurse; Health Dimensions Group appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Delta Medical Systems Notifies Patients About July 2025 Cyberattack
entry.content:
Data breaches have recently been announced by Delta Medical Systems in Wisconsin, Ansell Healthcare Products in New Jersey, and FuturHealth […] The post Delta Medical Systems Notifies Patients About July 2025 Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Trinity Health & UPMC Notify Patients About Potential Unauthorized Data Access via HIE
entry.content:
Trinity Health and the University of Pittsburgh Medical Center are notifying patients about potential unauthorized access to patient data by […] The post Trinity Health & UPMC Notify Patients About Potential Unauthorized Data Access via HIE appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required
entry.content:
According to the Paubox 2026 Healthcare Email Security Report, in 2025, 170 email-related data breaches were reported to the HHS’ […] The post Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: LeakNet ransomware uses ClickFix, Deno runtime in stealthy attacks
entry.content:
The LeakNet ransomware gang is now using the ClickFix technique for initial access into corporate environments and deploys a malware loader based on the open-source Deno runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Europe sanctions Chinese and Iranian firms for cyberattacks
entry.content:
The European Union Council has announced sanctions against three entities and two individuals for their involvement in cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure in the region. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New “Darksword” iOS exploit used in infostealer attack on iPhones
entry.content:
A new exploit kit for iOS devices and delivery framework dubbed "Darksword" has been used to steal a wide range of personal information, including data from cryptocurrency wallet app. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Marquis: Ransomware gang stole data of 672K people in cyberattack
entry.content:
Marquis, a Texas-based financial services provider, revealed this week that a ransomware gang stole the data of over 670,000 individuals in an August 2025 cyberattack that also disrupted operations at 74 banks across the United States. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Ransomware gang exploits Cisco flaw in zero-day attacks since January
entry.content:
The Interlock ransomware gang has been exploiting a maximum severity remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Cisco's Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) software in zero-day attacks since late January. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CISA orders feds to patch Zimbra XSS flaw exploited in attacks
entry.content:
CISA has ordered U.S. government agencies to secure their servers against an actively exploited vulnerability in the Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS). [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Aura confirms data breach exposing 900,000 marketing contacts
entry.content:
Identity protection company Aura has confirmed that an unauthorized party gained access to nearly 900,000 customer records containing names and email addresses. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.content:
